stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 3 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 5 observations with mean 63.8 % (within-trial site SD = 6.3). trial site B has 10 observations with mean 21.0 % (within-trial site SD = 6.6). trial site C has 5 observations with mean 53.2 % (within-trial site SD = 7.8). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.3 and 20.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 62.9 % (shrunk from raw 63.8). Range [61.3, 63.4] as between-trial site SD varies from 8.3 to 20.9. Pooled mean: 39.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 39.8 % (weighted average of 3 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=13.9: B = 0.961.
Interval [61.3, 63.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (63.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (39.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
